jobs.description: <div class="content-intro"><h2><strong>About Anthropic</strong></h2> <p>Anthropic’s mission is to create reliable, interpretable, and steerable AI systems. We want AI to be safe and beneficial for our users and for society as a whole. Our team is a quickly growing group of committed researchers, engineers, policy experts, and business leaders working together to build beneficial AI systems.</p></div><h2><strong>About the role</strong></h2> <p>As a Safeguards Policy Design Manager, you will be responsible for developing usage policies, clarifying enforcement guidelines, and advising on safety interventions for our products and services. Your core focus will be on age-appropriate design and experiences, including child safety, age assurance, content classification, and adult sexual content. You will help define best practices for developers building on claude for deployment to users across different developmental stages, design age-assurance policies that protect minors from inappropriate content and interactions, and establish clear boundaries for adult content and experiences. In addition, you will advise teams on opportunities for age-appropriate helpfulness, including advising cross-functional teams on beneficial use cases for younger users where appropriate. Safety is core to our mission and you’ll help shape policy creation and development so that our users can safely interact with and build on top of our products in a harmless, helpful and honest way.</p> <p><em>*Important context for this role: In this position you may be exposed to and engage with explicit content spanning a range of topics, including those of a sexual, violent, or psychologically disturbing nature.</em></p> <h2 class="heading"><strong>Responsibilities:</strong></h2> <ul> <li> <p>Serve as an internal subject matter expert, leveraging deep expertise in child safety, adult content, youth development, and age-appropriate design to:</p> <ul> <li> <p>Draft new policies that help govern the responsible use of our models for emerging capabilities and use cases</p> </li> <li> <p>Design evaluation frameworks for testing model performance in areas of expertise</p> </li> </ul> </li> <li> <p>Conduct regular reviews and testing of existing policies to identify and address gaps and ambiguities</p> </li> <li> <p>Review flagged content to drive enforcement and policy improvements</p> </li> <li> <p>Update our usage policies based on feedback collected from external experts, our enforcement team, and edge cases that you will review</p> </li> <li> <p>Work with safeguards product teams to identify and mitigate concerns, and collaborate on designing appropriate intervention

jobs.description: <div class="content-intro"><p><strong>Why join us</strong></p> <p>Brex is the AI-powered spend platform. We help companies spend with confidence with integrated corporate cards, banking, and global payments, plus intuitive software for travel and expenses. Tens of thousands of companies from startups to enterprises — including DoorDash, Flexport, and Compass — use Brex to proactively control spend, reduce costs, and increase efficiency on a global scale.</p> <p><span style="font-weight: 400;">Working at Brex allows you to push your limits, challenge the status quo, and collaborate with some of the brightest minds in the industry. jobs.description: <div class="content-intro"><p>Airtable is the no-code app platform that empowers people closest to the work to accelerate their most critical business processes. More than 500,000 organizations, including 80% of the Fortune 100, rely on Airtable to transform how work gets done.</p></div><p>Airtable is the AI-native app platform that empowers people closest to the work to accelerate their most critical business processes. More than 500,000 organizations, including 80% of the Fortune 100, rely on Airtable to transform how work gets done.</p> <p>Our Self Serve team owns the growth engine of Airtable - how people discover value, adopt AI native workflows, and become long-term customers. We focus on onboarding, activation, conversion, expansion, and monetization across the product experience.</p> <p>We’re looking for a Product Design Manager to lead design for Funnel &amp; Monetization. We're hiring a product design manager to lead this team while staying close to the craft of design: you'll split your time between people leadership and hands-on design. You’ll partner deeply across Product, Engineering, Data, and Marketing to create experiences that are intuitive, cohesive, and measurably effective.</p> <h1><strong>What you'll do</strong></h1> <h2>Lead and develop your team</h2> <ul> <li>Manage 1-3 product designers: give them clear ownership, direct feedback, and the support they need to grow and do their best work.</li> <li>Own your team's feedback culture. Set a high bar in a way that's constructive - your designers should know what good looks like and feel equipped to get there.</li> <li>Know each person's growth edges. Give feedback that's specific, create stretch opportunities, and invest in their development.</li> <li>Manage resourcing for your area: scope work to match capacity, flag tradeoffs early, and protect your team from overload.</li> </ul> <h2>Shape strategy with your cross-functional partners</h2> <ul> <li>Partner with PM and engineering leads to define roadmaps and success metrics, in addition to executing against them. Design has a voice in what and how something gets built.</li> <li>Use design-led exploration - early concepts, AI prototyping, jams - to help your team define the problem and solution space.</li> </ul> <h2>Contribute as an individual designer</h2> <ul> <li>Own end-to-end design for significant work in your area, from problem framing through final details, alongside your team's projects.</li> <li>Set the quality bar through your own output as much as through feedback.</li> <li>Stay close enough to the work that your perspective is sharp and grounded in the nuances of specific business and technical inputs.</li> </ul&g

We want someone who is passionate about our mission and who believes in the power of crypto and blockchain technology to update the financial system. We want someone who is eager to leave their mark on the world, who relishes the pressure and privilege of working with high caliber colleagues, and who actively seeks feedback to keep leveling up. jobs.description: <p data-pm-slice="1 1 []">RDQ427R273</p> <p><strong>Mission</strong></p> <p>At Databricks, our mission is to simplify and democratize data and AI so more organizations can solve the world’s toughest problems. As a Product Designer focused on AI/BI experiences, your mission is to make advanced analytics, generative AI, and business intelligence intuitive, trustworthy, and actionable for both technical and non-technical users.</p> <p>As a Product Designer focused on AI/BI experiences, you won’t just design dashboards or workflows. You’ll design how humans and AI think together.</p> <p>You’ll help define what it feels like to ask a question of your company’s data in plain language — and receive an answer you can trust. You’ll shape how insights are generated, explained, challenged, and shared. You’ll craft the bridge between complex AI systems and real human decisions.&nbsp;You'll be working closely with the product and engineering teams based in Seattle.</p> <p><strong>The impact you will have:</strong></p> <ul> <li>Reimagine how users explore data using natural language and AI assistance</li> <li>Design interactions that make AI-generated insights transparent and trustworthy</li> <li>Create experiences that move from exploration → validation → action in minutes</li> <li>Enable collaboration between data engineers, analysts, and business leaders</li> <li>Help define new design patterns for AI-native enterprise software</li> <li>Influence long-term AI/BI strategy, contributing to how Databricks differentiates in the modern analytics landscape.</li> </ul> <p><strong>What we look for:</strong></p> <ul> <li>5+ years of product design work experience.</li> <li>A bachelor's degree or equivalent, with specialization in HCI, Interaction Design, graphic design, computer science, or related disciplines.</li> <li>You’re comfortable talking to strategic customers in key decision-making positions.</li> <li>Experience shipping great digital products. We strongly believe that a designer’s involvement never stops. You should be comfortable overseeing and impacting the entire process from idea to GA.</li> <li>Demonstrated ability to lead large and complex design projects and balance the needs of diverse stakeholders.</li> <li>Can execute beautiful visual and interaction work that’s rooted in a data-driven and well-researched UX process.</li> <li>A system thinker who has the vision to design the big picture, and the tactical ability to break it down so that engineering can succeed in building it incrementally.</li> <li>You have a unique combination of technical knowledge and visual design skills, which allows you to design powerful and intuitive engi

jobs.title: Sr. Product Designer, Data Engineering
jobs.description: <p data-pm-slice="1 1 []">RDQ327R173</p> <p><strong>Mission</strong></p> <p>At Databricks, we design tools that make complex data workflows intuitive and powerful. On the data engineering team, our mission is to simplify how data is ingested, transformed, and prepared so that teams across an organization can turn raw data into impact. Designers at Databricks blend strong visual craft with systems thinking, shaping products that support some of the most advanced data teams in the world.</p> <p>Lakeflow Connect solves a persistent data engineering problem: moving data from enterprise systems (Salesforce, Workday, SQL Server, and more) into Databricks. Over 2,000 customers already use our point-and-click ingestion experience, and we're hiring a Senior Product Designer to take it further.</p> <p>You'll own the end-to-end design of how users discover, configure, monitor, and troubleshoot their ingestion pipelines, shaping experiences for both data engineers and business analysts. You'll partner with cross-functional teams in SF and Seattle to ensure a cohesive experience across the data engineering journey.</p> <p><strong>The impact you will have</strong></p> <ul> <li>Design the front door to the Lakehouse. Your work will shape the first impressions and ongoing trust for thousands of organizations using Lakeflow Connect for enterprise data ingestion.</li> <li>Create experiences that serve both data engineers needing granular control and analysts wanting ready-to-use data</li> <li>Own the end-to-end ingestion experience across connector setup, credentials, scheduling, monitoring, error recovery, and schema evolution.</li> <li>Define AI-native data ingestion by integrating Databricks Assistant into pipeline creation and troubleshooting.</li> <li>Create scalable patterns for a diverse and growing range of connectors&nbsp;</li> </ul> <p><strong>What we look for</strong></p> <ul> <li>5+ years of product design work experience</li> <li>A bachelor's or higher degree in Design, HCI, or a related field, or equivalent practical experience.</li> <li>A strong portfolio of past projects demonstrating a solid design process, from inception to production</li> <li>Experience working collaboratively in a multidisciplinary team, communicating effectively with both design and non-design stakeholders.</li> <li>Can execute beautiful visual and interaction work that’s rooted in a data-driven and well-researched UX process</li> <li>Experience designing enterprise software, services, or other complex systems (Products in the Big Data space are a nice-to-have)</li> <li>Experience with React, SQL, CSS, and/or Python is a nice-to-have</li> </ul><div class="c

Product Designers at Databricks balance the creativity of a craftsperson with the analytical curiosity of a data scientist. We design simple and collaborative products that have the power to delight a highly technical audience.</p> <p>In this role, you’ll play a key part in shaping the future of Databricks design. We’re looking for an experienced designer to create the next generation of Databricks experiences across our platform. You are a great fit if you have the ability to define a strategic vision and collaborate with interdisciplinary teams to bring this vision to life.</p> <p>You will contribute to critical product areas, including Developer Experience (Notebooks), Data Governance (Unity Catalog), AI/Machine Learning, and Internal Tools. Your work will streamline how our users interact with, manage, and derive value from the Databricks Lakehouse Platform.</p> <p>You'll be working closely with the product and engineering teams based in SF/Seattle.</p> <p><strong>The impact you will have:</strong></p> <ul> <li>Design best-in-class user experiences across core areas of the Databricks platform.</li> <li>Help identify and establish common design patterns for a consistent platform experience.</li> <li>Drive product design for critical business areas at Databricks.</li> <li>Design and launch new experiences that simplify complex workflows for data practitioners and developers.</li> <li>Collaborate closely with product management, engineering, and leadership to shape the future of our platform.</li> <li>Design intuitive user experiences that align with engineering best practices, simplifying complex workflows for our users.</li> <li>Explore new ways in which Generative AI can enhance the platform experience and developer workflows.</li> <li>Develop a deep understanding of Databricks business objectives, the cloud and big data space, its users, and competition.</li> <li>Conduct user research to identify customer needs and pain points related to data science and platform usage.</li> </ul> <p><strong>What we look for:</strong></p> <ul> <li>5+ years of product design work experience.</li> <li>A bachelor's degree or equivalent, with specialization in HCI, Interaction Design, graphic design, computer science, or related disciplines.&

jobs.title: Senior / Staff Product Designer
jobs.description: At Linear, we're building the product development system for teams and agents. AI is fundamentally changing how software gets built, and we’re shaping the tools this new era requires. Founded in 2019, Linear has become the platform of choice for more than 25,000 companies (including OpenAI, Coinbase, and Ramp) to plan, build, and ship their products. Today, our team is distributed across North America and Europe, and we’re continuing to grow internationally. What unites us is relentless focus, fast execution, and a deep care for software craftsmanship. Quality is our competitive advantage. Every member of our fully remote team is a maker at heart, caring deeply about the quality and feel of our work. While the industry optimizes for speed and metrics alone, we believe that craft and quality have lasting value. Quality creates gravity — it pulls people toward our team and product rather than requiring us to push. This philosophy drives everything we do, from product decisions to hiring choices. For this role, we expect robust design skills, sharp product thinking, and the ability to engage in technical discussions. We work in small, autonomous project teams, where engineers are paired tightly with designers to explore ideas, build prototypes, deploy internal builds, and ultimately ship to customers. You will be a key element of projects from beginning to end. Location & work mode Linear is a remote-first company. This role is open to candidates based in North America. You can work from anywhere within this region. We value deep focus and async collaboration, with intentional moments to connect in person through team off-sites, optional co-working, and occasional travel. What you'll do - Pair closely with engineering and product to initiate and complete roadmap projects, no hand-offs - Spot opportunities to redesign or refine key screens and flows, as well as smaller quality issues and “paper cuts” as you use Linear - Create mockups, prototypes, and high-fidelity visuals to communicate ideas and describe their execution - Guide and inspire fellow designers through collaborative critique of work-in-progress - Use and improve our lightweight design system components - Help with hiring new teammates that can elevate our team and product What we’re looking for - 7+ years experience designing software - Courage to ask questions, experiment, and advocate for what you feel is right - Humility to accept feedback, adapt your thinking, and iterate toward the best solution - Experience with prototyping in Figma (and beyond with tools like AI, HTML/CSS, Origami) - Ability to distill problems and feedback, then design clear, focused solutions - Effective written and verbal communication skills — much of our work is async What we offer We're a small, focused team that cares deeply about the quality of our work and the people we do it with.
